title: westringia fruticosa
intro: Westringia fruticosa, also known as Coast Rosemary or Native Rosemary, is a shrub native to Australia and Southeast Asia, characterized by its small, white or purple flowers and silvery-green foliage. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial effects, making it useful for treating various health issues. The health benefits of Westringia fruticosa include reducing stress and anxiety, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. Westringia fruticosa is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and essential oils, and can also be consumed as a supplement or used topically in skincare products.
